title: Step 1: Add Reset Button UI to Maintenance Management (no logic)

requirements.summary: Add an admin-only, visually destructive Reset button to the MaintenanceManagement component header without any click handler logic
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reset' button is visible in the MaintenanceManagement component when the logged-in user is an admin.
The button is NOT visible to non-admin (viewer) users.
The button uses a destructive or warning visual style (red/orange tones) to signal danger.
The button is placed near the existing admin controls (e.g., next to or below the 'Add Maintenance Expense' button).
Clicking the button does not trigger any backend call or data mutation — it is a no-op or logs a placeholder console message.
No new backend functions or mutations are introduced in this step.
The rest of the MaintenanceManagement UI remains unchanged.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Reset' button to the component's header/controls area that: (1) is conditionally rendered only when isAdmin is true (using the same pattern as the existing 'Add Maintenance Expense' button), (2) uses a destructive/warning visual style (e.g., variant='destructive' or custom red/orange Tailwind classes), (3) is positioned near the existing admin controls for visual consistency, (4) has an onClick handler that either does nothing or logs a placeholder console message (e.g., console.log('Reset button clicked - no logic wired yet')), and (5) leaves all existing functionality and state management unchanged. Use the authorization component's role-check pattern already present in the component to guard the button visibility.
